![Darren Verlenden_CEO_Single Use Support-1](/uploads/media/article_latest/02/3452-Darren%20Verlenden_CEO_Single%20Use%20Support-1.jpg?v=1-0 "Darren Verlenden_CEO_Single Use Support-1")
![](/build/website/svg/shield.57c1fac1.svg)

# Case Study: Reducing product loss | Cost-efficiency

![Claus Exenberger - Single Use Support](/uploads/media/blog_avatar/06/2036-CLEX.jpg?v=4-0 "Claus Exenberger - Single Use Support")

[Claus Exenberger](/author/claus-exenberger "Claus Exenberger")

June 8, 2022

Table of contents

**Biopharmaceutical companies have largely accepted a certain level of [product loss](https://www.susupport.com/knowledge/biopharmaceutical-products/vaccines/loss-drug-substance-challenges-chances-product-loss) when managing drug substances in single-use systems - unaware that solutions exist to streamline process steps and dramatically diminish product loss . The breaking of single-use bags in turn means [loss of very valuable drug substances](https://www.susupport.com/knowledge/biopharmaceutical-products/vaccines/loss-drug-substance-challenges-chances-product-loss).**

A global biotechnology company produces different drug substances of highly valuables monoclonal antibodies (mABs). The company experienced a product loss rate between 0.5% and 5% due to the sensitivity of single-use bioprocess containers at sub-zero temperatures. Therefore, the **goal was to reduce the current product loss below 0.5%.**

This case study discloses how the implementation of RoSS® technologies from Single Use Support had an impact on the product loss during trials of hundreds of single-use bags.

You will be surprised of the cost savings due to reduced product loss.

Case Study - Reducing Product Loss | Cost-Efficiency

Did you enjoy this article? Share it with others!

![Claus Exenberger - Single Use Support](/uploads/media/blog_avatar/06/2036-CLEX.jpg?v=4-0 "Claus Exenberger - Single Use Support")

[Claus Exenberger](/author/claus-exenberger "Claus Exenberger")

Product Line Management

Claus Exenberger is Product Line Manager at Single Use Support. As product owner of the [RoSS shell](https://www.susupport.com/solutions/consumables/non-sterile-consumables/ross-protect-single-use-bag), the secondary packaging of single-use bags, Claus and his team develops further non-sterile products to advance handling of single-use bags for the biopharmaceutical industry. With his working experience of 5+ years at Single Use Support he is expert in the protection of all available single-use bioprocess containers. His proximity to customers helps connect his process-oriented approach with the technical development of products.

## More from Claus Exenberger

![RoSS with frozen IRIS Bag (10 von 10)-min](/uploads/media/blog_tiles/01/961-RoSS%20with%20frozen%20IRIS%20Bag%20%2810%20von%2010%29-min.jpg?v=1-0 "RoSS with frozen IRIS Bag (10 von 10)-min")

Monoclonal Antibodies

### [Safe handling of monoclonal antibodies: Best practices](https://www.susupport.com/knowledge/biopharmaceutical-products/monoclonal-antibodies/safe-handling-monoclonal-antibodies-best-practices "Safe handling of monoclonal antibodies: Best practices")

Monoclonal antibodies bring along some special requirements in terms of safe handling. In this article, we will discover what these requirements are, and how they can be met.

![Claus Exenberger - Single Use Support](/uploads/media/blog_avatar/06/2036-CLEX.jpg?v=4-0 "Claus Exenberger - Single Use Support")

Claus Exenberger

February 7, 2024

![primary-secondary-packaging](/uploads/media/blog_tiles/04/1374-primary-secondary-packaging.png?v=2-0 "Primary and secondary packaging of drug substances")

Single-use Technology

### [Primary and secondary packaging of drug substances](https://www.susupport.com/knowledge/manufacturing-processes/single-use-technology/primary-secondary-packaging-drug-substances "Primary and secondary packaging of drug substances")

Throughout the manufacturing process of drug substances, the liquids must be transferred frequently, such as during the upstream and downstream processing. Safe packaging of large volume substances is of high necessity in each step. Protection is ensured by appropriate primary and secondary packaging. This article will provide an overview of the different types of primary and secondary packaging materials, which are used specifically for safe handling of drug substance.

![Claus Exenberger - Single Use Support](/uploads/media/blog_avatar/06/2036-CLEX.jpg?v=4-0 "Claus Exenberger - Single Use Support")

Claus Exenberger

March 14, 2023

## Read more about Single-use Technology

![RoSS.FILL - single use filling machine for CGT bags](/uploads/media/blog_tiles/03/143-RoSS.FILL%20CGT_Product%20photo%20%285%20von%2026%29.jpg?v=2-0 "RoSS.FILL machine for CGT bags")

Single-use Technology

### [Go with The Flow: How Sustainable is the Trend Toward Single-use Systems?](https://www.susupport.com/knowledge/manufacturing-processes/single-use-technology/with-flow-sustainable-trend-toward-single-use-systems "Go with The Flow: How Sustainable is the Trend Toward Single-use Systems?")

Single-use systems support novel approaches to aseptic fluid management and aseptic liquid transfer. However, regarding the sustainability of single-use systems, there are still concerns within the scientific community. BioPharm International sat down with Single Use Support to address these apprehensions and discuss the future of single-use systems in biopharma.

![Khalil Essani - Single Use Support](/uploads/media/blog_avatar/04/2034-KHES.jpg?v=4-0 "Khalil Essani - Single Use Support")

Khalil Essani

February 5, 2024

![Frozen IRIS Single-Use Bag in RoSS (4)](/uploads/media/blog_tiles/08/1408-Frozen%20IRIS%20Single-Use%20Bag%20in%20RoSS%20%284%29.jpg?v=1-0 "Frozen IRIS Single-Use Bag in RoSS (4)")

Single-use Technology

### [Blocking UV Light from Biopharmaceuticals](https://www.susupport.com/knowledge/manufacturing-processes/single-use-technology/blocking-light-from-biopharmaceuticals "Blocking UV Light from Biopharmaceuticals")

New on the market are single-use bags that are designed to block ultraviolet (UV) light. By utilizing cold chain packagings that effectively block UV light, manufacturers can maintain the integrity and quality of their products throughout the entire bioprocessing chain.

![1640281789974](/uploads/media/blog_avatar/02/1522-DASE.jpg?v=3-0 "1640281789974")

David Seifert

July 26, 2024

![RoSS protecting single-use bags](/uploads/media/blog_tiles/01/451-ross-protecting-single-use-bags.jpg?v=1-0 "RoSS protecting single-use bags")

Single-use Technology

### [How to avoid single-use bag leakages in bioprocessing](https://www.susupport.com/knowledge/manufacturing-processes/single-use-technology/avoid-single-use-bag-leakages-bioprocessing "How to avoid single-use bag leakages in bioprocessing")

Single-use bag leakage and the resulting product loss can cause delays in life-saving treatments, safety risks as well as financial loss. Therefore, this article will discuss aspects to consider in order to keep single-use bags safe and sound.

![micheal-eder](/uploads/media/blog_avatar/07/47-MIED.jpg?v=3-0 "micheal-eder")

Michael Eder

November 14, 2023

![Bioprocess single-use technology Advancements](/uploads/media/blog_tiles/09/2219-IRIS%20Single-Use%20Assmeblies_Production%20Kufstein-37.jpg?v=1-0 "Bioprocess single-use technology Advancements")

Single-use Technology

### [Bioprocess solutions: Advancements with single-use technology](https://www.susupport.com/knowledge/manufacturing-processes/single-use-technology/bioprocess-solutions-advancements-with-single-use-technology "Bioprocess solutions: Advancements with single-use technology")

Bioprocess manufacturing has seen remarkable advancements through the utilization of single-use technology. This shift in process development brings numerous advantages – in this article, we will cover some major advances.

![michael-muehlegger](/uploads/media/blog_avatar/06/46-MIMU.jpg?v=3-0 "michael-muehlegger")

Michael Mühlegger

November 13, 2023

Let's create a great future together!

# Case Study – Technology Transfer Process Optimization in a Pharmaceutical Company

![](https://www.pharmacontinuum.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/08/tech_transfer.png)

#### Case Study – Technology Transfer Process Optimization in a Pharmaceutical Company

In the pharmaceutical industry, the robustness of the **Technology Transfer (TT)** process plays a pivotal role in ensuring regulatory compliance, product quality, and time-to-market efficiency. In accordance with ICH Q10 and WHO guidelines on technology transfer, companies are expected to implement structured, science- and risk-based approaches to manage the transfer of manufacturing processes and analytical methods.

In this context, we conducted a targeted project within a mid-sized **Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization (CDMO)** to assess and optimize their TT framework. The objective was to map the current process, identify inefficiencies, and create a strategic, resource-based roadmap to enhance TT performance.

## **Methodology Overview**

The project was structured into five key phases, aligned with industry best practices and regulatory expectations:

Technology Transfer is not just about moving documents and equipment—it’s about transferring knowledge in a controlled, reproducible way. A robust TT process must anticipate variability, align cross-functional teams early, and ensure traceability and compliance from day one

## **Results and Strategic Impact**

This structured evaluation led to the identification of **15 critical improvement opportunities**, several of which had a **cross-functional nature** affecting project planning, analytical readiness, and manufacturing coordination. The proposed changes ranged from procedural updates to improved communication flows, data handover practices, and earlier involvement of QA in TT milestones.

The final roadmap, supported by senior management, defined clear responsibilities and a phased implementation plan. As a result, the company expects to:

## **Regulatory Alignment and Compliance**

The project explicitly referenced:

## **Conclusion**

Technology Transfer is not merely a technical task—it’s a strategic capability. By applying a lean, structured approach and engaging all relevant functions, this CDMO was able to transform its TT process from a reactive system into a proactive, value-adding function. The method is scalable, compliant, and ready to support rapid innovation and client expectations in a competitive pharmaceutical landscape.

![](https://www.pharmacontinuum.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Yellow-and-Brown-Illustrative-Business-Roadmap-Poster.png)

enriched_points.expanded_content: Case studies selected must directly apply to biopharmaceutical processes, addressing challenges like product loss and efficiency in drug manufacturing, ensuring tangible insights for industry practices.
Chosen case studies should showcase measurable improvements in productivity and quality, utilizing metrics such as reduced costs and enhanced yield to validate methodologies applied.

The selected cases must incorporate cutting-edge technologies, such as single-use systems and process analytical technologies, demonstrating their role in optimizing bioprocess control and performance.
